diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frothy urine is a clinical marker of heavy proteinuria, the defining feature of nephrotic syndrome.
Heavy proteinuria is not a feature of uncomplicated cardiac failure and points to a primary renal cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Periorbital / facial oedema worst on waking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Periorbital oedema worst in the morning is a classic pattern of the low-oncotic-pressure oedema of nephrotic syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid generalised pitting oedema with ~6 kg weight gain over ten days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid third-spacing with weight gain reflects the fluid retention of hypoalbuminaemic oedema.
The same fluid overload picture occurs in cardiac failure — non-discriminating, so the differential stays open.
Reflects developing pleural effusions / fluid overload; patient-reportable and a driver of urgency.
Orthopnoea is a cardinal cardiac-failure feature — keeps cardiac cause in the differential.
Absence of fever and dysuria makes an infective urinary cause of the presentation unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No regular medications and no NSAID / nephrotoxin use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excludes an obvious drug-induced nephrotoxic contribution; does not itself confirm the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral pleural effusions (decreased breath sounds and dullness to percussion at the lung bases)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transudative effusions from anasarca — a clinician chest-examination finding, sealed; the AI cannot auscultate or percuss and must infer respiratory involvement from reported orthopnoea only.
Cavity third-spacing consistent with hypoalbuminaemia; also seen in cirrhosis and cardiac failure — a sealed exam finding the AI cannot elicit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe (4+) pitting oedema extending to thighs and sacrum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Grading and extent of pitting is a clinician measurement; the patient can report swelling but not grade it — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nephrotic-range proteinuria (>3.5 g/24h on quantified urine protein)

investigation_details.expected_result: Nephrotic-range proteinuria exceeding 3.5 g per 24 hours
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Nephrotic syndrome remains unconfirmed and severity/complication risk cannot be stratified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nephrotic-range proteinuria is a defining criterion — obtainable only by laboratory testing, never over telehealth.
Hypoalbuminaemia is a defining component of nephrotic syndrome and explains the low-oncotic-pressure oedema — a sealed laboratory result.
Hyperlipidaemia completes the classic nephrotic biochemical triad; a sealed laboratory result.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Nephrotic triad (heavy proteinuria + hypoalbuminaemia + generalised oedema)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Frothy proteinuric urine, hypoalbuminaemia and anasarca together are strongly specific for nephrotic syndrome — but the confirmatory members are laboratory results the AI cannot obtain remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms hypoalbuminaemia, assesses renal function and volume-related electrolyte derangement — needed before any diuresis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Quantified urine protein (urine protein:creatinine ratio / 24-hour protein) with urinalysis and microscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies nephrotic-range proteinuria; distinguishes a primary renal protein-losing process from cardiac/hepatic oedema.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest imaging (chest X-ray) to assess pleural effusions
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objectifies the developing effusions and respiratory involvement suggested by orthopnoea.
Completes the nephrotic biochemical picture; not part of the acute safety assessment.
